summary: The Schedule tab is a comprehensive glassmorphism-redesigned job scheduler for the Cadi cleaning business SaaS. It provides 5 interactive views (Day, Week, Month, Quarter, Rounds) with live job management, crew-based filtering, and drag-to-reorder functionality. The Day view is the hero interface showing per-crew swim lanes on a 20-minute time grid with colour-coded job types (residential green, commercial blue, exterior orange).
features: 5 scheduler views (Day/Week/Month/Quarter/Rounds)
Per-crew swim lanes with 20-minute granularity
Drag-to-reorder jobs with real-time start_hour persistence
Colour-coded job types (residential/commercial/exterior/site_visit)
Status tracking (scheduled/in-progress/complete/unassigned)
Stat strip showing total jobs, revenue, completion counts
Type + crew filters with dynamic pill UI
Job creation modal with service picker, recurrence scheduling, staff assignment
Job drawer with status updater, notes editor, delete confirmation
Mobile day-strip quick navigation
Week view with overlapping job layout engine
Month view calendar grid with job dots and revenue per day
Quarter view with 13-week revenue bar chart and completion tracking
Rounds view for imported recurring customer rounds with schedule info
Search by customer name or postcode
Route button to Google Maps for incomplete jobs in Day view
Live Supabase sync with realtime updates
Demo mode for unauthenticated users
dataModel: jobs (id, owner_id, customer_id, customer, date, start_hour, duration_hrs, type, service, price, status, assignee, assignees, notes, postcode, address_line1, address_line2, town, county, recurrence, source)
customer_rounds (id, business_id, customer_id, round_name, schedule, price_per_visit, due_date, account_status, notes)
connections: DataContext (useData hook) provides live jobs, customers, CRUD handlers (addJobAndSyncCustomer, updateJob, deleteJob)
AuthContext (useAuth hook) for user authentication and profile data (service types, home postcode)
AskCadi component for AI assistant integration
RoutePlanner tab receives jobs and handles mileage logging
CustomerImport component can navigate to Scheduler
App.jsx mounts at /scheduler route and lazy-loads SchedulerPreview demo at /scheduler-preview

summary: CRM dashboard for managing cleaning business customers with profile view, AI-powered upsell suggestions, secure vault for access codes, direct debit integration, and bulk import wizard. Built to scale from 10 to 10,000+ records using client-side filtering; free tier capped at 30 customers, Pro unlimited.
features: Customer list with density-aware rendering (large/medium/compact by customer count)
Search by name, postcode, email, notes (instant client-side)
Filter by status (active/lapsed/at-risk/archived), segment (commercial/residential/exterior), job type (12 types)
Sort by name, lifetime value, last job date, or urgency (AI-scored)
AI suggestion engine: upsells, cross-sells, win-back reminders, lapsed customer recovery (rules-based, no API calls for speed)
Customer detail panel: overview, job history, invoices, suggestions, message templates, secure vault
Add/edit customer modal with frequency, status, segment, notes, star rating
Message composer with template library (win-back, reminder, upsell offers, AI personalization via Claude API)
Secure vault (PIN-protected access codes storage with staff access logging, auto-lock 30s)
GoCardless Direct Debit integration: sync customer, create mandate link, collect payments
Commercial customer surveys (link to /survey route)
Customer import wizard: CSV/Excel from QuickBooks, CleanerPlanner, Squeegee, Aworka, Jobber, ServiceM8, Google Sheets (4-step: source → upload → column map → preview → done)
Bulk import job scheduling from CleanerPlanner/Squeegee: auto-detects format, maps rounds, schedules across 4-month window
Plan enforcement: free tier limits to 30 customers, upgrade modal on cap
Archived customer view (soft delete)
Portal invite CTA (stub: 'Coming soon')
Archive/remove button with confirmation
dataModel: customers (Supabase): id, owner_id, name, email, phone, address_line1/2, town, county, postcode, frequency, status (active/lapsed/at-risk/archived), tags, notes, source, rating, service_types, last_job_date, next_job_date, lifetime_value, due_date, job_reference, customer_reference, schedule, customer_balance, price_per_visit, round_name, account_status, segment (residential/commercial/exterior/unsegmented), segment_source
customer_rounds (Supabase): id, business_id, customer_id, job_reference, round_name, schedule, price_per_visit, due_date, account_status, notes

jobs (Supabase): id, owner_id, customer_id, customer, address (line1/2, town, county, postcode), date, start_hour, duration_hrs, type (residential/exterior/commercial), service, price, status, assignee, assignees, assignee_ids, recurrence, notes, source, created_at
invoices (Supabase): id, customer_id, invoice_num, date, status (draft/sent/paid/overdue), lines (array), paid_at, payment_method
surveys (Supabase): id, customer_id, status (capturing/structured/quoted/archived), created_at, customers (join)
connections: DataContext (useData hook): reads/writes customers, triggers addCustomer/updateCustomer/deleteCustomer, refreshCustomers/refreshJobs
AuthContext (useAuth hook): retrieves user.id for owner_id
usePlan hook: isPro, customerLimit flags for free/pro tier enforcement
Supabase client: direct queries for jobs, invoices, surveys (customer detail); edge function for AI message generation (ai-generate), GoCardless API (gocardless-api)
customersDb.js: listCustomers, upsertCustomer, archiveCustomer
customerRoundsDb.js: listRoundsForCustomer, bulkInsertRounds, deleteRoundsForCustomer, updateRound
jobsDb.js: bulkCreateJobs (for import)
surveyDb.js: createSurvey, listSurveysForCustomer, listOpenSurveys
Router: navigates to /scheduler (book job), /survey/{id} (commercial surveys)
CustomerImport component (sibling): imports customers from CSV/Excel, returns via onImported/onViewScheduler callbacks

deadEnds.what: Customer portal invite button: 'Coming soon' — no onclick, no link
Quick action: 'Call' button with no onClick handler
Quick action: 'Add note' button with no onClick handler

duplicates.what: Archive confirmation logic repeated 3 times (full card, medium card, compact card density)
GlassCard background + grid texture pattern copy-pasted across modals
PIN pad keypad logic (Secure Vault: unlock numpad, verify PIN against VAULT_STAFF array) — hardcoded staff PINs
Button styling for py-2.5 + rounded-xl + border + transition appears 30+ times (not componentized)
Message template generation: static templates + AI generation via generateMessage() and generateAIMessage()

summary: Services tab is the core service menu editor in the Cadi cleaning SaaS, allowing business owners to define, price, and manage their service offerings across residential, exterior, and commercial categories. It integrates with Front Desk for quoting, uses a Supabase backend for persistence, and provides both a conversational AI builder (ServiceChat) and traditional form interface for service configuration.
features: Service CRUD (create, read, update, delete, duplicate)
Multi-category organization (Residential, Exterior, Commercial)
Dynamic pricing configuration with six pricing methods (per_size, hourly, fixed, per_sqm, per_room, custom)
Service catalogue quick-add from predefined templates
Inline active/inactive toggle with toast notifications
Conditional pricing warnings (yellow alert when pricing missing)
Frequency options (one-off, weekly, fortnightly, monthly, quarterly, annually)
Conversational AI service builder (Cadi) with quick/thorough modes
Service duplication feature
First-visit banner explaining service menu importance
Attention banner tracking services needing pricing
Front Desk preview mode
Service area override (default vs custom postcodes)
Materials & equipment notes field
Private notes field
Site visit required toggle
Description included/excluded fields
Pricing notes for Front Desk contextualization
dataModel: services (Supabase table) — primary table with fields: id, category, name, description_included, description_excluded, pricing_type, pricing_matrix, price_hourly_rate, price_hourly_minimum_hours, price_fixed_basic, price_fixed_standard, price_fixed_premium, price_per_sqm, price_per_sqm_minimum, price_per_room, price_per_bathroom, pricing_notes, duration_value, duration_unit, frequency_one_off, frequency_weekly, frequency_fortnightly, frequency_monthly, frequency_quarterly, frequency_annually, service_area_uses_default, service_area_custom, materials_equipment_notes, private_notes, is_active, site_visit_required, display_order, created_at
profiles (Supabase, referenced for seeding) — setup_data.services and setup_data.custom_service during first setup
connections: Front Desk preview (FrontDeskPreview component) — shows how this service menu will appear to customers
ServiceChat (conversational builder) — alternative interface to form-based service creation; can edit existing services or build new ones
Settings page (Settings.jsx imports PricingSettings) — separate pricing rule configuration system for pricing_rules and pricing_addons tables (not directly Services, but related)
Onboarding flow (seedServicesFromOnboarding via setup_data) — auto-imports services selected during signup if user has no services yet
Calculator tab (PricingCalculator) — consumes services for quick-quote UI on residential/commercial/exterior pricing
